name: PyTorch on Apple M2: which device do I pick?

chat_messages.text: I'm setting up PyTorch on an M2 MacBook Pro for some local model fine-tuning. Should I use mps or cpu? My model is a small transformer (~50M params).

For a 50M-param transformer on M2, use `torch.device("mps")`. You'll get roughly 4-6x speedup over CPU on the unified memory architecture. Two gotchas: 1) some ops fall back to CPU silently — set `PYTORCH_ENABLE_MPS_FALLBACK=1` so you don't crash mid-training. 2) install via the conda nightly channel: `conda install pytorch torchvision torchaudio -c pytorch-nightly`.
Got it. Any reason to NOT use mps?

Three: (1) if you need bitsandbytes 8-bit quantization — it's CUDA-only. (2) if your model uses ops not yet ported (some attention variants, sparse tensors). (3) if you're benchmarking against a CUDA reference — the numerics are slightly different.

name: Why did my weight rebound after 6 months on keto?

chat_messages.text: I lost 12kg on a strict keto diet over 6 months but in the last month my weight is creeping back up even though I'm still under 30g carbs/day. What's going on?

Three likely mechanisms: (1) Adaptive thermogenesis — your BMR drops 10-15% during weight loss as the body conserves energy. After 6 months you're burning ~200-300 fewer kcal/day at rest than at the start. (2) Calorie creep — fat-fueled meals are calorie-dense (avocado, nuts, oils); satiety adapts and portions drift up unconsciously. (3) Insulin sensitivity recovery — improved insulin response means the small glucose loads you DO get are stored more efficiently. Track 7 days of intake honestly and compare to your TDEE calculator output minus 15%.
I haven't been tracking, just eyeballing. So you're saying eyeballing stops working?

Yes, and it's the most common pattern. Eyeballing works during the novelty phase because your brain is hyper-vigilant. After 4-6 months the same foods feel "smaller" portion-wise even when objectively the same — sensory-specific satiety adapts. Re-introduce one week of measuring and the gap usually reveals itself.

name: Best chunk size for RAG over a personal note vault

chat_messages.text: I'm building a RAG system over my Obsidian vault (~2000 notes, average ~600 words each). What chunk size should I use for the embeddings?

For ~600-word notes I'd skip chunking entirely — embed each note as a single vector. Reasoning: (1) bge-m3 handles 8192 tokens; your average note is ~800 tokens. (2) Chunking destroys document-level context; "the medication" in paragraph 3 loses the antecedent in paragraph 1. (3) Reranking after retrieval is much more accurate when the candidate IS the document.
But what about really long notes, like multi-thousand-word essays?

For >2000-word notes, split on H2 headings, not arbitrary char count. Heading-aware splits respect the author's logical sections. Add the H1 title as a prefix on every chunk so the embedder sees "Essay on X — Section Y" not just the bare section text.
What about overlap between chunks?
Skip overlap. It bloats the index 30%+ for marginal recall gains; the reranker covers the boundary case better.

name: Pricing tiers for an indie SaaS — 3 vs 4 plans?

chat_messages.text: Launching a small B2B SaaS for solo developers. Should I have 3 pricing tiers or 4? Currently planning Free / $19 / $49 / $99.

Cut to 3: Free / $19 / $49. Drop the $99. Reason: with 4 tiers people anchor on the middle two and the top tier eats decision-time without converting much. The classic Williams-Sonoma bread-machine effect (introduce a $429 model, $279 model sells more) only works with physical goods where context comparison is fast. SaaS users open a comparison table and stall.
What if the $99 tier is the one I actually want most people to buy?

Then make it Free / $49 / $99 and use $99 as the default-highlighted middle option. "Most popular" badge on it. Free is for evaluation; $49 is the budget-conscious choice; $99 reads as the "obvious" pick because it's flanked.
And the upgrade path from free to paid?

One feature gate, not a usage gate. Usage gates feel punitive ("you've used 80%, upgrade now"). Feature gates feel aspirational ("unlock team workspace"). Pick the most loved feature and put it behind the wall.

name: Log vs Rec.709 for narrative video edit

chat_messages.text: Shooting a short doc with the FX3. Is the workflow easier if I shoot Rec.709 directly or stay in S-Log3 and grade later?

Stay in S-Log3 unless you're delivering the same day. The dynamic range gain (~14 stops vs 10) is significant in narrative — you'll thank yourself the first time you have to recover detail in a window or a face. The grade overhead is one LUT pass + a node tree you build once and reuse.
What's the LUT to start from?

Sony's S-Log3 to Rec.709 conversion LUT (free, official). Apply it as the first node in DaVinci Resolve, then do creative grading downstream. Don't bake the LUT into your camera files — keep them log on disk.
Storage cost difference between Log and Rec.709?
Same — they're both 4:2:2 10-bit XAVC-I at 600Mbps on the FX3. The picture profile is metadata + tone curve, not a different codec.
I want to build an agent that takes meeting notes and stores them in my vault via MCP. What's the minimum tool set?

Three tools cover 90% of the use case: (1) `transcribe_audio(path) -> text` — pipe to Whisper or a hosted ASR. (2) `search_vault(query) -> list[note]` — to detect duplicates and link related notes. (3) `write_note(path, body, frontmatter)` — the actual write. The agent loop: transcribe → search for related → draft note → search again to dedupe → write.
Should the agent be allowed to OVERWRITE existing notes?

No. Append-only or append-with-section-stub-pointing-at-original. Overwriting destroys recoverable state without an undo path. If the agent thinks it's updating an existing note, it should write `note-v2.md` and let the user merge — diffs are easier than archeology.

name: Renal-support diet for an older cat

chat_messages.text: My 14-year-old cat just got diagnosed with early CKD (stage 2). Vet recommended renal-support food. Are the prescription brands actually different from regular senior food, or is it marketing?

Genuinely different on three axes: (1) Phosphorus restricted to ~0.4-0.6% (vs ~1.0% in regular senior food) — phosphorus retention is the main driver of CKD progression. (2) Higher quality protein, slightly reduced quantity — the goal is to reduce urea nitrogen load without inducing muscle wasting. (3) Added omega-3s (EPA/DHA) for anti-inflammatory effect on the kidney. The marketing is real on this one.
What if she refuses to eat the renal food?

Common — renal foods taste worse to cats because palatability tradeoffs come with the formulation. Try: (1) warming wet food to body temp, (2) topping with low-sodium tuna juice (drained), (3) gradual transition over 14 days mixing 25% / 50% / 75% / 100%. If she still won't eat after 2 weeks, ask the vet about Aluvia or Epakitin (phosphate binders) so you can stick with food she'll actually eat.

name: Are AI subscriptions tax-deductible for a sole trader?

chat_messages.text: I'm a sole-trader software developer in Australia. Can I claim my Claude Pro and ChatGPT Plus subscriptions as work expenses?

Yes, fully deductible to the extent of business use, same as any software subscription. Keep evidence of business use (link to a project, a code commit, a client deliverable) per ATO substantiation rules. If you use the same accounts personally too, apportion: 70% business / 30% personal is a common defensible split for general-purpose tools. Save the receipts via the provider's billing page.
What about the API costs? I pay per-token via OpenRouter for an internal tool I'm building.

100% deductible — usage-based API spend on a tool you're building is unambiguous business expense. OpenRouter sends a monthly invoice; download those PDFs and tag them in your accounting software under "Software & subscriptions" or "R&D" depending on whether the tool is core product or research.

name: DIY sensor cleaning vs sending to service

chat_messages.text: I have visible dust spots on my Sony A7IV sensor. Should I send it to Sony service or buy a sensor cleaning kit?

Buy a kit. Sony service runs ~$80-120 + 2 weeks downtime; a VSGO sensor cleaning kit is ~$30 and the technique is straightforward. The risk people fear (scratching the sensor) is overstated — modern sensors have a glass low-pass filter; the swab + solvent doesn't touch the silicon.
What's the actual procedure?

(1) Set the camera to manual sensor cleaning mode (Menu → Setup → Cleaning Mode). (2) Detach lens, point camera down. (3) Two drops of Eclipse fluid on the swab — not soaking wet. (4) ONE pass left-to-right with even pressure, then flip the swab and ONE pass right-to-left. (5) Re-test by shooting a clear sky at f/16 and checking for spots.

name: Should my puzzle game have a hint system?

chat_messages.text: I'm designing a logic puzzle game (think Baba Is You / Patrick's Parabox style). Should I include a hint system, and if so, how visible should it be?

Include hints, but make them tiered and progressive. Three levels works: (1) Nudge — "Have you tried interacting with the blue block?". (2) Direction — "You need to push the blue block onto the switch first.". (3) Solution — full step-by-step. Lock each tier behind a 2-3 minute cooldown so the player can't just spam-skip. Visibility: a small "?" icon in the corner, never an unsolicited "need help?" popup. Unsolicited help shame-flags the player.
What about achievements for solving without hints?

Yes, but per-puzzle, not per-game. Per-game achievements ("finish the whole game with no hints") are completionist bait that makes players replay the entire game just to chase a checkmark — they'll spoiler themselves on YouTube to avoid restarting from a single hint use. Per-puzzle achievements are achievable and replayable in isolation.
